About.Name: Dat Thai
About.AboutText:
Hi, I am Dat Thai. I am a passionate electrical engineering student with a keen interest in circuits. Currently, I am pursuing my undergraduate degree at Seattle Pacific University. I am dedicated to expanding my knowledge and skills in various areas of electrical engineering.

I enjoy tackling challenging projects and thrive in collaborative environments where I can contribute my expertise and learn from my peers.

Experience.ExperienceText: Electrical Engineering student at Seattle Pacific University with hands-on experience in circuit design and debugging.
Experience.EducationText: Pursuing a Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering at Seattle Pacific University.
Experience.SkillsText: Skilled in analog circuit design, debugging, and test equipment.

Project1.Title: Peak Detector Circuit
Project1.Desc:
Designed and built a peak detector circuit using op-amps, a diode, and a capacitor to detect the peak voltage of an input signal.

The circuit uses an LM339 comparator to compare the peak detector output against a reference voltage, triggering an LED indicator when the signal exceeds the threshold. Tested and debugged at frequencies ranging from 10kHz to 25kHz.
